docs.text: Sherlock Distributed Lock ¶ Distributed lock JVM library ¶ Sherlock Distributed Lock is a JVM library that provides distributed locking mechanism. It uses a database to store locks and exposes both synchronous and reactive API. It was created as a simple solution to manage distributed locks among multiple microservices. Name Sherlock comes from words "shared lock". The problem ¶ Distribute locking may be achieved through multiple mechanisms: Locking with service discovery like Zookeeper Locking in SQL transaction Locking with atomic find and modify database actions This library was designed to implement the last option. Lock is acquired with an atomic update. To solve the problem of not released lock (because of a lock owner failure) locks are acquired for a specific duration. When time passes, lock is expired and is automatically released. Quick start ¶ Add dependency to build.gradle : dependencies { compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-mongo-sync:0.4.1" } Create synchronous lock: // Get mongo locks collection String database = "sherlock" ; MongoClient mongoClient = MongoClients . create ( "mongodb://localhost:27017/" + database ); MongoCollection < Document > collection = mongoClient . getDatabase ( "sherlock" ) . getCollection ( "locks" ); // Create sherlock Sherlock sherlock = MongoSherlock . builder () . withLocksCollection ( collection ) . build (); // Create a lock DistributedLock lock = sherlock . createLock ( "sample-lock" ); Acquire a lock: // Acquire a lock if ( lock . acquire ()) { try { System . out . println ( "Lock granted!" ); } finally { lock . release (); } } ...or acquire a lock in a more concise way: lock . acquireAndExecute (() -> { System . out . println ( "Lock granted!" ); }); Learn more Learn hot use different APIs and connectors .
docs.title: Home

docs.text: Add dependency to build.gradle : dependencies { compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-mongo-sync:0.4.1" } Create synchronous lock: // Get mongo locks collection String database = "sherlock" ; MongoClient mongoClient = MongoClients . create ( "mongodb://localhost:27017/" + database ); MongoCollection < Document > collection = mongoClient . getDatabase ( "sherlock" ) . getCollection ( "locks" ); // Create sherlock Sherlock sherlock = MongoSherlock . builder () . withLocksCollection ( collection ) . build (); // Create a lock DistributedLock lock = sherlock . createLock ( "sample-lock" ); Acquire a lock: // Acquire a lock if ( lock . acquire ()) { try { System . out . println ( "Lock granted!" ); } finally { lock . release (); } } ...or acquire a lock in a more concise way: lock . acquireAndExecute (() -> { System . out . println ( "Lock granted!" ); }); Learn more Learn hot use different APIs and connectors .
docs.title: Quick start

docs.text: Sherlock API ¶ Sherlock exposes synchronous and reactive API in order to fit any code base. Exposed APIs: Synchronous API - Synchronous API that uses blocking database connectors Reactor API - Reactive API that uses Reactor and reactive database connectors RxJava API - Reactive API that uses RxJava (v2) and reactive database connectors
docs.title: API

docs.text: Sherlock exposes synchronous and reactive API in order to fit any code base. Exposed APIs: Synchronous API - Synchronous API that uses blocking database connectors Reactor API - Reactive API that uses Reactor and reactive database connectors RxJava API - Reactive API that uses RxJava (v2) and reactive database connectors
docs.title: Sherlock API

docs.text: Sherlock Connectors ¶ Sherlock multiple database connectors to fit in your infrastructure. Provided connectors: Mongo Connector - Uses MongoDB and its JVM drivers to store locks SQL Connector - Uses SQL database (tested on Postgres and MySQL ) and JDBC drivers to store locks In-Memory Connector - Stores locks in memory. Created for local development and testing.
docs.title: Connectors

docs.text: Sherlock multiple database connectors to fit in your infrastructure. Provided connectors: Mongo Connector - Uses MongoDB and its JVM drivers to store locks SQL Connector - Uses SQL database (tested on Postgres and MySQL ) and JDBC drivers to store locks In-Memory Connector - Stores locks in memory. Created for local development and testing.
docs.title: Sherlock Connectors

docs.text: Sherlock Installation ¶ Different APIs and Connectors require different dependencies defined in build.gradle . Installing synchronous Sherlock with MongoDB Connector : dependencies { compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-mongo-sync:0.4.1" } Installing reactive Sherlock with Reactor API and MongoDB Connector : dependencies { compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-mongo-reactive:0.4.1" compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-api-reactor:0.4.1" } Installing reactive Sherlock with RxJava API and MongoDB Connector : dependencies { compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-mongo-reactive:0.4.1" compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-api-rxjava:0.4.1" } Configuration looks similar for different connectors and can be generalized into: // synchronous dependencies { compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-$CONNECTOR-sync:0.4.1" } // reactor api dependencies { compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-$CONNECTOR-reactive:0.4.1" compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-api-reactor:0.4.1" } // rxjava api dependencies { compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-$CONNECTOR-reactive:0.4.1" compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-api-rxjava:0.4.1" }
docs.title: Installation

docs.text: Different APIs and Connectors require different dependencies defined in build.gradle . Installing synchronous Sherlock with MongoDB Connector : dependencies { compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-mongo-sync:0.4.1" } Installing reactive Sherlock with Reactor API and MongoDB Connector : dependencies { compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-mongo-reactive:0.4.1" compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-api-reactor:0.4.1" } Installing reactive Sherlock with RxJava API and MongoDB Connector : dependencies { compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-mongo-reactive:0.4.1" compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-api-rxjava:0.4.1" } Configuration looks similar for different connectors and can be generalized into: // synchronous dependencies { compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-$CONNECTOR-sync:0.4.1" } // reactor api dependencies { compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-$CONNECTOR-reactive:0.4.1" compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-api-reactor:0.4.1" } // rxjava api dependencies { compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-$CONNECTOR-reactive:0.4.1" compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-api-rxjava:0.4.1" }
docs.title: Sherlock Installation

docs.text: Lock Duration ¶ There are 3 methods for acquiring a lock: lock.acquire() - acquires lock for a default duration (5 minutes) after which lock is automatically released lock.acquire(Duration.ofMinutes(3)) - acquires a lock for a specific duration lock.acquireForever() - acquires a lock forever. Use it wisely.
docs.title: Lock Duration

docs.text: There are 3 methods for acquiring a lock: lock.acquire() - acquires lock for a default duration (5 minutes) after which lock is automatically released lock.acquire(Duration.ofMinutes(3)) - acquires a lock for a specific duration lock.acquireForever() - acquires a lock forever. Use it wisely.
docs.title: Lock Duration

docs.text: Sherlock Lock Types ¶ SingleEntrantDistributedLock ¶ Owner of a SingleEntrantDistributedLock cannot acquire the same lock twice. Acquiring lock with SingleEntrantDistributedLock : DistributedLock lock = sherlock . createLock ( "single-entrant" ); Instance A | Instance B assert lock . acquire () == true ; | | assert lock . lock () == false assert lock . acquire () == false ;| Releasing SingleEntrantDistributedLock (the same as ReentrantDistributedLock ): Instance A | Instance B assert lock . acquire () == true ; | | assert lock . lock () == false | assert lock . release () == false assert lock . release () == true ; | assert lock . release () == false ;| ReentrantDistributedLock ¶ Owner of a ReentrantDistributedLock can acquire the same lock multiple times Acquiring ReentrantDistributedLock : DistributedLock lock = sherlock . createReentrantLock ( "reentrant" ); Instance A | Instance B assert lock . acquire () == true ; | | assert lock . lock () == false assert lock . acquire () == true ; | Releasing ReentrantDistributedLock (the same as SingleEntrantDistributedLock ): Instance A | Instance B assert lock . acquire () == true ; | | assert lock . lock () == false | assert lock . release () == false assert lock . release () == true ; | assert lock . release () == false ;| OverridingDistributedLock ¶ OverridingDistributedLock lock may be acquired and/or released any time. It was created for purely administrative tasks, like releasing a lock that was blocked in acquired state. Acquiring a OverridingDistributedLock DistributedLock lock = sherlock . createOverridingLock ( "overriding" ); Instance A | Instance B assert lock . acquire () == true ; | | assert lock . lock () == true assert lock . acquire () == true ; | assert lock . acquire () == true ; | Releasing a OverridingDistributedLock Instance A | Instance B assert lock . acquire () == true ; | | assert lock . lock () == true | assert lock . release () == true assert lock . release () == false ;| assert lock . release () == false ;|
docs.title: Lock Types

docs.text: Owner of a SingleEntrantDistributedLock cannot acquire the same lock twice. Acquiring lock with SingleEntrantDistributedLock : DistributedLock lock = sherlock . createLock ( "single-entrant" ); Instance A | Instance B assert lock . acquire () == true ; | | assert lock . lock () == false assert lock . acquire () == false ;| Releasing SingleEntrantDistributedLock (the same as ReentrantDistributedLock ): Instance A | Instance B assert lock . acquire () == true ; | | assert lock . lock () == false | assert lock . release () == false assert lock . release () == true ; | assert lock . release () == false ;|
docs.title: SingleEntrantDistributedLock

docs.text: Owner of a ReentrantDistributedLock can acquire the same lock multiple times Acquiring ReentrantDistributedLock : DistributedLock lock = sherlock . createReentrantLock ( "reentrant" ); Instance A | Instance B assert lock . acquire () == true ; | | assert lock . lock () == false assert lock . acquire () == true ; | Releasing ReentrantDistributedLock (the same as SingleEntrantDistributedLock ): Instance A | Instance B assert lock . acquire () == true ; | | assert lock . lock () == false | assert lock . release () == false assert lock . release () == true ; | assert lock . release () == false ;|
docs.title: ReentrantDistributedLock

docs.text: OverridingDistributedLock lock may be acquired and/or released any time. It was created for purely administrative tasks, like releasing a lock that was blocked in acquired state. Acquiring a OverridingDistributedLock DistributedLock lock = sherlock . createOverridingLock ( "overriding" ); Instance A | Instance B assert lock . acquire () == true ; | | assert lock . lock () == true assert lock . acquire () == true ; | assert lock . acquire () == true ; | Releasing a OverridingDistributedLock Instance A | Instance B assert lock . acquire () == true ; | | assert lock . lock () == true | assert lock . release () == true assert lock . release () == false ;| assert lock . release () == false ;|
docs.title: OverridingDistributedLock

docs.text: Migrator ¶ Incubating feature Sherlock migrator is an incubating feature. Its API may change. It is implemented only for synchronous connectors. Lock based migration process ¶ Distributed locks may be used for multiple purposes one of them is a one way database migration process: // prepare the migration SherlockMigrator migrator = new SherlockMigrator ( "db-migration" , sherlock ) . addChangeSet ( "add db index" , () -> /* ... */ ) . addChangeSet ( "remove stale collection" , () -> /* ... */ ) // run the migrtion migrator . migrate (); Migration rules: migrations must not be run in parallel (neither by one nor by multiple machines) migration change sets are applied in order migration change set must be run only once per all migrations migration process stops when first change set fails Migration mechanism is in experimental phase. It is available only in via sherlock-sync API.
docs.title: Migrator

docs.text: Incubating feature Sherlock migrator is an incubating feature. Its API may change. It is implemented only for synchronous connectors.
docs.title: Migrator

docs.text: Distributed locks may be used for multiple purposes one of them is a one way database migration process: // prepare the migration SherlockMigrator migrator = new SherlockMigrator ( "db-migration" , sherlock ) . addChangeSet ( "add db index" , () -> /* ... */ ) . addChangeSet ( "remove stale collection" , () -> /* ... */ ) // run the migrtion migrator . migrate (); Migration rules: migrations must not be run in parallel (neither by one nor by multiple machines) migration change sets are applied in order migration change set must be run only once per all migrations migration process stops when first change set fails Migration mechanism is in experimental phase. It is available only in via sherlock-sync API.
docs.title: Lock based migration process

docs.text: Testing ¶ In Memory Connector The easiest way to setup Sherlock in tests is to use In-Memory Connector . Use stubs when you need more control over the locking mechanism. For easy stubbing and mocking exposed API consists mostly of interfaces (see: Sherlock , ReactorSherlock , DistributedLock , ReactorDistributedLock ). The library provides also stubs or mocks. See: SherlockStub , ReactorSherlockStub , DistributedLockMock and ReactorDistributedLockMock . Sample usage in spock tests: def "should release a lock after operation" () { given: "there is a released lock" DistributedLockMock lock = DistributedLockMock . alwaysReleasedLock () when: "single instance action is executed" boolean taskPerformed = singleInstanceAction ( lock ) then: "the task was performed" taskPerformed == true and: "lock was acquired and released" lock . wasAcquiredAndReleased == true } def "should not perform single instance action when lock is locked" () { given: "there is a lock acquired by other instance" DistributedLockMock lock = DistributedLockMock . alwaysAcquiredLock () when: "single instance action is executed" boolean taskPerformed = singleInstanceAction ( lock ) then: "action did not perform the task" taskPerformed == false and: "action failed acquiring the lock" lock . wasAcquireRejected == true and: "action did not release the lock" lock . wasReleaseInvoked == false }
docs.title: Testing

docs.text: In Memory Connector The easiest way to setup Sherlock in tests is to use In-Memory Connector . Use stubs when you need more control over the locking mechanism. For easy stubbing and mocking exposed API consists mostly of interfaces (see: Sherlock , ReactorSherlock , DistributedLock , ReactorDistributedLock ). The library provides also stubs or mocks. See: SherlockStub , ReactorSherlockStub , DistributedLockMock and ReactorDistributedLockMock . Sample usage in spock tests: def "should release a lock after operation" () { given: "there is a released lock" DistributedLockMock lock = DistributedLockMock . alwaysReleasedLock () when: "single instance action is executed" boolean taskPerformed = singleInstanceAction ( lock ) then: "the task was performed" taskPerformed == true and: "lock was acquired and released" lock . wasAcquiredAndReleased == true } def "should not perform single instance action when lock is locked" () { given: "there is a lock acquired by other instance" DistributedLockMock lock = DistributedLockMock . alwaysAcquiredLock () when: "single instance action is executed" boolean taskPerformed = singleInstanceAction ( lock ) then: "action did not perform the task" taskPerformed == false and: "action failed acquiring the lock" lock . wasAcquireRejected == true and: "action did not release the lock" lock . wasReleaseInvoked == false }
docs.title: Testing

docs.text: Distributed Lock with Reactor API ¶ Add dependencies to build.gradle : dependencies { compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-mongo-reactive:0.4.1" compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-api-reactor:0.4.1" } Create reactive lock with Reactor API: // Get mongo locks collection String database = "sherlock" ; MongoClient mongoClient = MongoClients . create ( "mongodb://localhost:27017/" + database ); MongoCollection < Document > collection = mongoClient . getDatabase ( database ) . getCollection ( "locks" ); // Create sherlock ReactorSherlock sherlock = ReactiveMongoSherlock . builder () . withLocksCollection ( collection ) . buildWithApi ( ReactorSherlock :: reactorSherlock ); // Create a lock with reactor api ReactorDistributedLock lock = sherlock . createLock ( "sample-lock" ); Acquire a lock: // Acquire a lock lock . acquire () . filter ( LockResult :: isLocked ) . flatMap ( result -> { System . out . println ( "Lock granted!" ); return lock . release (); }) . block (); ...or shorter lock . acquireAndExecute (() -> Mono . just ( "Lock granted!" )) . block ();
docs.title: Reactor

docs.text: Add dependencies to build.gradle : dependencies { compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-mongo-reactive:0.4.1" compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-api-reactor:0.4.1" } Create reactive lock with Reactor API: // Get mongo locks collection String database = "sherlock" ; MongoClient mongoClient = MongoClients . create ( "mongodb://localhost:27017/" + database ); MongoCollection < Document > collection = mongoClient . getDatabase ( database ) . getCollection ( "locks" ); // Create sherlock ReactorSherlock sherlock = ReactiveMongoSherlock . builder () . withLocksCollection ( collection ) . buildWithApi ( ReactorSherlock :: reactorSherlock ); // Create a lock with reactor api ReactorDistributedLock lock = sherlock . createLock ( "sample-lock" ); Acquire a lock: // Acquire a lock lock . acquire () . filter ( LockResult :: isLocked ) . flatMap ( result -> { System . out . println ( "Lock granted!" ); return lock . release (); }) . block (); ...or shorter lock . acquireAndExecute (() -> Mono . just ( "Lock granted!" )) . block ();
docs.title: Distributed Lock with Reactor API

docs.text: Distributed Lock with RxJava API ¶ Add dependencies to build.gradle : dependencies { compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-mongo-reactive:0.4.1" compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-api-rxjava:0.4.1" } Create reactive lock with RxJava API: // Get mongo locks collection String database = "sherlock" ; MongoClient mongoClient = MongoClients . create ( "mongodb://localhost:27017/" + database ); MongoCollection < Document > collection = mongoClient . getDatabase ( database ) . getCollection ( "locks" ); // Create sherlock RxSherlock sherlock = ReactiveMongoSherlock . builder () . withLocksCollection ( collection ) . buildWithApi ( RxSherlock :: rxSherlock ); // Create a lock with reactor api RxDistributedLock lock = sherlock . createLock ( "sample-lock" ); Acquire a lock: // Acquire a lock lock . acquire () . filter ( LockResult :: isLocked ) . flatMap ( result -> { System . out . println ( "Lock granted!" ); return lock . release (); }) . blockingGet (); ...or shorter lock . acquireAndExecute ( Single . fromCallable (() -> { logger . info ( "Lock acquired!" ); return true ; })). blockingGet ();
docs.title: RxJava

docs.text: Add dependencies to build.gradle : dependencies { compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-mongo-reactive:0.4.1" compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-api-rxjava:0.4.1" } Create reactive lock with RxJava API: // Get mongo locks collection String database = "sherlock" ; MongoClient mongoClient = MongoClients . create ( "mongodb://localhost:27017/" + database ); MongoCollection < Document > collection = mongoClient . getDatabase ( database ) . getCollection ( "locks" ); // Create sherlock RxSherlock sherlock = ReactiveMongoSherlock . builder () . withLocksCollection ( collection ) . buildWithApi ( RxSherlock :: rxSherlock ); // Create a lock with reactor api RxDistributedLock lock = sherlock . createLock ( "sample-lock" ); Acquire a lock: // Acquire a lock lock . acquire () . filter ( LockResult :: isLocked ) . flatMap ( result -> { System . out . println ( "Lock granted!" ); return lock . release (); }) . blockingGet (); ...or shorter lock . acquireAndExecute ( Single . fromCallable (() -> { logger . info ( "Lock acquired!" ); return true ; })). blockingGet ();
docs.title: Distributed Lock with RxJava API

docs.text: Synchronous Distributed Lock ¶ Add dependency to build.gradle : dependencies { compile "com.coditory.sherlock:sherlock-mongo-sync:0.4.1" } Create synchronous lock: // Get mongo locks collection String database = "sherlock" ; MongoClient mongoClient = MongoClients . create ( "mongodb://localhost:27017/" + database ); MongoCollection < Document > collection = mongoClient . getDatabase ( "sherlock" ) . getCollection ( "locks" ); // Create sherlock Sherlock sherlock = MongoSherlock . builder () . withLocksCollection ( collection ) . build (); // Create a lock DistributedLock lock = sherlock . createLock ( "sample-lock" ); Acquire a lock: // Acquire a lock if ( lock . acquire ()) { try { System . out . println ( "Lock granted!" ); } finally { lock . release (); } } ...or acquire a lock in a more concise way: lock . acquireAndExecute (() -> { System . out . println ( "Lock granted!" ); });
docs.title: Synchronous

docs.text: In Memory Distributed Lock ¶ In Memory connector was created for local development and testing purposes. Synchronous in-memory sherlock ¶ Sherlock sherlock = inMemorySherlockBuilder () . withClock ( Clock . systemDefaultZone ()) . withUniqueOwnerId () . withSharedStorage () . build (); // ...or simply // Sherlock sherlockWithDefaults = inMemorySherlock(); Learn more See the full sample on Github , read sherlock builder javadoc . Reactive in-memory sherlock ¶ ReactorSherlock sherlock = reactiveInMemorySherlockBuilder () . withClock ( Clock . systemDefaultZone ()) . withUniqueOwnerId () . withSharedStorage () . buildWithApi ( ReactorSherlock :: reactorSherlock ); // ...or simply // ReactorSherlock sherlockWithDefaults = reactorSherlock(reactiveInMemorySherlock()); Learn more See the full sample on Github , read sherlock builder javadoc . RxJava version can be created in a similar way, see the sample on Github .
docs.title: InMemory

docs.text: In Memory connector was created for local development and testing purposes.

docs.text: Mongo Distributed Lock ¶ MongoDB connector enables distributed locking on MongoDB . It was tested on MongoDB v3.4 . Locks collection ¶ Sample lock document: { "_id" : "lock-id" , "acquiredBy" : "owner-id" , "acquiredAt" : { "$date" : 1562502838189 }, "expiresAt" : { "$date" : 1562503458189 } } Synchronous MongoDB Sherlock ¶ Creating synchronous mongo sherlock: String database = "sherlock" ; MongoClient mongoClient = MongoClients . create ( "mongodb://localhost:27017/" + database ); MongoCollection < Document > locksCollection = mongoClient . getDatabase ( database ) . getCollection ( "locks" ); Sherlock sherlock = mongoSherlock () . withClock ( Clock . systemDefaultZone ()) . withLockDuration ( Duration . ofMinutes ( 5 )) . withUniqueOwnerId () . withLocksCollection ( locksCollection ) . build (); // ...or simply // Sherlock sherlockWithDefaults = mongoSherlock(locksCollection); Learn more See the full sample on Github , read sherlock builder javadoc . Reactive MongoDB Sherlock ¶ Creating reactive mongo sherlock: String database = "sherlock" ; MongoClient mongoClient = MongoClients . create ( "mongodb://localhost:27017/" + database ); MongoCollection < Document > locksCollection = mongoClient . getDatabase ( database ) . getCollection ( "locks" ); ReactorSherlock sherlock = reactiveInMemorySherlockBuilder () . withClock ( Clock . systemDefaultZone ()) . withUniqueOwnerId () . withSharedStorage () . withLocksCollection ( locksCollection ) . buildWithApi ( ReactorSherlock :: reactorSherlock ); // ...or simply // ReactorSherlock sherlockWithDefaults = reactorSherlock(reactiveInMemorySherlock(locksCollection)); Learn more See the full sample on Github , read sherlock builder javadoc . RxJava can be created in a similar way, see the sample on Github .
docs.title: MongoDb

docs.text: MongoDB connector enables distributed locking on MongoDB . It was tested on MongoDB v3.4 .

docs.title: Locks collection

docs.text: Creating synchronous mongo sherlock: String database = "sherlock" ; MongoClient mongoClient = MongoClients . create ( "mongodb://localhost:27017/" + database ); MongoCollection < Document > locksCollection = mongoClient . getDatabase ( database ) . getCollection ( "locks" ); Sherlock sherlock = mongoSherlock () . withClock ( Clock . systemDefaultZone ()) . withLockDuration ( Duration . ofMinutes ( 5 )) . withUniqueOwnerId () . withLocksCollection ( locksCollection ) . build (); // ...or simply // Sherlock sherlockWithDefaults = mongoSherlock(locksCollection); Learn more See the full sample on Github , read sherlock builder javadoc .
docs.title: Synchronous MongoDB Sherlock

docs.text: Creating reactive mongo sherlock: String database = "sherlock" ; MongoClient mongoClient = MongoClients . create ( "mongodb://localhost:27017/" + database ); MongoCollection < Document > locksCollection = mongoClient . getDatabase ( database ) . getCollection ( "locks" ); ReactorSherlock sherlock = reactiveInMemorySherlockBuilder () . withClock ( Clock . systemDefaultZone ()) . withUniqueOwnerId () . withSharedStorage () . withLocksCollection ( locksCollection ) . buildWithApi ( ReactorSherlock :: reactorSherlock ); // ...or simply // ReactorSherlock sherlockWithDefaults = reactorSherlock(reactiveInMemorySherlock(locksCollection)); Learn more See the full sample on Github , read sherlock builder javadoc . RxJava can be created in a similar way, see the sample on Github .
docs.title: Reactive MongoDB Sherlock

docs.text: SQL Distributed Lock ¶ SQL connector enables distributed locking on a relational databases. It was tested on Postrgres v11 and MySQL v8 . Locks Table ¶ Locks table is automatically created if it did not already exist. Table is created with a following SQL: CREATE TABLE LOCKS ( ID VARCHAR ( 100 ) NOT NULL , ACQUIRED_BY VARCHAR ( 100 ) NOT NULL , ACQUIRED_AT TIMESTAMP ( 3 ) NOT NULL , EXPIRES_AT TIMESTAMP ( 3 ), PRIMARY KEY ( ID ) ) Table name may be changed during sherlock creation. Synchronous SQL Sherlock ¶ Synchronous API only SQL connector provides synchronous API only. There is no reliable reactive jdbc driver. Properties connectionProps = new Properties (); connectionProps . put ( "user" , "mysql" ); connectionProps . put ( "password" , "mysql" ); Connection dbConnection = return DriverManager . getConnection ( "jdbc:mysql://localhost:${mysql.firstMappedPort}/mysql" , connectionProps ); Sherlock sherlock = sqlSherlock () . withClock ( Clock . systemDefaultZone ()) . withLockDuration ( Duration . ofMinutes ( 5 )) . withUniqueOwnerId () . withConnection ( dbConnection ) . withLocksTable ( "LOCKS" ) . build (); // ...or simply // Sherlock sherlockWithDefaults = sqlSherlock(dbConnection); Learn more See the full sample on Github , read sherlock builder javadoc .
docs.title: SQL

docs.text: SQL connector enables distributed locking on a relational databases. It was tested on Postrgres v11 and MySQL v8 .

Table is created with a following SQL: CREATE TABLE LOCKS ( ID VARCHAR ( 100 ) NOT NULL , ACQUIRED_BY VARCHAR ( 100 ) NOT NULL , ACQUIRED_AT TIMESTAMP ( 3 ) NOT NULL , EXPIRES_AT TIMESTAMP ( 3 ), PRIMARY KEY ( ID ) ) Table name may be changed during sherlock creation.
docs.title: Locks Table

docs.text: Synchronous API only SQL connector provides synchronous API only. There is no reliable reactive jdbc driver. Properties connectionProps = new Properties (); connectionProps . put ( "user" , "mysql" ); connectionProps . put ( "password" , "mysql" ); Connection dbConnection = return DriverManager . getConnection ( "jdbc:mysql://localhost:${mysql.firstMappedPort}/mysql" , connectionProps ); Sherlock sherlock = sqlSherlock () . withClock ( Clock . systemDefaultZone ()) . withLockDuration ( Duration . ofMinutes ( 5 )) . withUniqueOwnerId () . withConnection ( dbConnection ) . withLocksTable ( "LOCKS" ) . build (); // ...or simply // Sherlock sherlockWithDefaults = sqlSherlock(dbConnection); Learn more See the full sample on Github , read sherlock builder javadoc .
docs.title: Synchronous SQL Sherlock
